name: Lonavala
bestMonths: Jun–Sep for green and waterfalls, Oct–Feb for pleasant weather
summary: The classic monsoon hill station: misty ghats, waterfalls, lakes and chikki. About 2 hours from Mumbai on the Expressway, and barely an hour from your Pune house.

highlights.name: Lion's Point
highlights.note: Sweeping valley views at golden hour. Often fogged in during peak monsoon, which is its own kind of magic.

highlights.name: Bhushi Dam
highlights.note: Stepped dam that overflows in monsoon. Beautiful but crowded and slippery, go early and stay off the fast water.

highlights.name: Tiger's Leap
highlights.note: A dramatic cliff drop with a seasonal waterfall across the valley.

highlights.name: Karla Caves
highlights.note: Ancient rock-cut Buddhist caves. A good indoor-ish option if it pours.

highlights.name: Lonavala Lake
highlights.note: Calm and brimming green in the rains.

name: Karjat
bestMonths: Jun–Sep for rivers and green, year-round for farm stays
summary: Riverside and farm-stay country at the foot of the Sahyadris. Closest of the lot to Mumbai, lush and full of water in the monsoon.

highlights.name: Kondana Caves trail
highlights.note: A green walk to rock-cut caves with a monsoon waterfall. Slippery, wear grippy shoes.

highlights.name: Ulhas river point
highlights.note: Riverside spots run strong in monsoon; stay on the banks.

highlights.name: ND Studios tour
highlights.note: A film-set studio tour, fun and dry on a wet day.

highlights.name: Farm-stay pool and lawns
highlights.note: Most of Karjat's charm is a good farm stay with views.

name: Mulshi
bestMonths: Jun–Sep for full lakes and waterfalls
summary: Lake-and-dam country an hour from your Pune house, so it makes most sense as a Pune-side trip. Tamhini Ghat nearby turns into a wall of waterfalls in the rains.

highlights.name: Mulshi Dam and lake
highlights.note: Vast lake ringed by green hills, calm and uncrowded on weekdays.

highlights.name: Tamhini Ghat waterfalls
highlights.note: A ghat road lined with waterfalls in monsoon. Drive slow, fog and water on the road.

highlights.name: Temghar backwaters
highlights.note: Quiet backwater views, beautiful in the rains.

name: Malshej Ghat
bestMonths: Jul–Sep is the whole point: waterfalls, mist and flamingos
summary: The monsoon spot. A mountain pass that becomes a curtain of waterfalls and mist in the rains, with pink flamingos at the dam. Limited stays, so book ahead.

highlights.name: Malshej waterfalls stretch
highlights.note: Roadside falls everywhere in monsoon. Stunning, but the road is narrow and wet, drive carefully.

highlights.name: Pimpalgaon Joga Dam (flamingos)
highlights.note: Flamingos gather here in the rains.

highlights.name: Harishchandragad base
highlights.note: Serious trek; only with a guide and not in heavy rain.

name: Mahabaleshwar
bestMonths: Oct–May for clear views; Jun–Sep for green and waterfalls
summary: Maharashtra's classic hill station — strawberry farms, viewpoints over the Sahyadris, and a gentler pace. Much closer from your Pune house than from Mumbai.

highlights.name: Arthur's Seat viewpoint
highlights.note: A dramatic edge above the valleys. Gets foggy in monsoon, which is part of its mystique — but wet rocks are slippery.

highlights.name: Venna Lake boat ride
highlights.note: A calm lake ringed by trees. Boat rides, horse rides, some quiet.

highlights.name: Mapro Garden strawberry farm
highlights.note: Fresh strawberry with cream, jams, crushes — the quintessential Mahabaleshwar stop.

highlights.name: Wilson's Point sunrise
highlights.note: The only east-facing viewpoint here — sunrise over the valley. Go early, it's worth the 5am alarm.

highlights.name: Pratapgad Fort
highlights.note: A historic Maratha fort on a hill, lush green in monsoon. About 25 km from town.

name: Alibaug
bestMonths: Oct–Feb for calm sea and beach walks; monsoon for lush forts but rough water
summary: The closest coastal escape from Mumbai — a quick ferry ride from the Gateway or an hour on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway and then NH66 south. Beach, colonial forts, and seafood.

highlights.name: Alibaug Beach and fort ruins
highlights.note: Walk the beach at low tide to see the Kolaba Fort ruins. No swimming in monsoon, but the walk is beautiful.

highlights.name: Kashid Beach day trip
highlights.note: 40 km south of Alibaug, Kashid is quieter and cleaner. Go early to beat the crowd.

highlights.name: Murud-Janjira sea fort
highlights.note: The only sea fort in Asia never to be conquered. A boat ride out to it is half the experience.

highlights.name: Varsoli beach sunset
highlights.note: The quieter, cleaner Alibaug beach — less crowd, great for a golden-hour walk.

name: Nashik
bestMonths: Oct–Mar for harvest and pleasant weather; monsoon grapes don't need tourists
summary: India's wine country — Sula Vineyards, Grover Zampa, and a handful of boutique wineries scattered across the hills outside town. Add Trimbak temple and Panchvati for a fuller trip.

highlights.name: Sula Vineyards tasting
highlights.note: The flagship Nashik winery — tasting session, vineyard walk, rooftop bar overlooking the vines. Book the tasting ahead.

highlights.name: York Winery lunch
highlights.note: Smaller and quieter than Sula, with a beautiful terrace restaurant.

highlights.name: Trimbakeshwar temple
highlights.note: One of the 12 Jyotirlinga — a significant Shiva temple 28 km from Nashik. Dress modestly.

highlights.name: Pandavleni Caves
highlights.note: Ancient Buddhist rock-cut caves on a hillside, 3rd century BCE. A quiet morning climb.

name: Bhandardara
bestMonths: Jul–Sep is the whole point: full lake, fireflies at night, waterfalls
summary: A lake-and-dam escape in the Sahyadris — full and green in the monsoon, with firefly walks at night in June–July. Randha Falls roars in the rains.

highlights.name: Arthur Lake viewpoint
highlights.note: The reservoir fills beautifully in monsoon. Sunrise from the dam is spectacular.

highlights.name: Randha Falls
highlights.note: Roaring waterfall in monsoon. Keep a safe distance — the gorge runs fast.

highlights.name: Firefly walk (June–July only)
highlights.note: Bhandardara is one of the best spots in Maharashtra to see fireflies. Night walk after dinner.

highlights.name: Wilson Dam spillway walk
highlights.note: When the dam overflows the spillway run is hypnotic. Views all the way to the hills.

eat: a farm kitchen near Bhandardara village

name: Goa
bestMonths: Nov–Feb for parties and sun; Oct for quiet and green after the rains
summary: When you want to go bigger — Goa is a 2-night minimum. Fly in 1 hour, or drive the NH66 in a day from Mumbai. Old Goa churches, Anjuna flea market, North Goa beach cafés, seafood dinners.

highlights.name: Anjuna or Vagator sunset at a cliffside café
highlights.note: North Goa's clifftop cafés at sunset — Curlies, Nine Bar, Hilltop. The sea goes gold.

highlights.name: Old Goa basilicas and Se Cathedral
highlights.note: UNESCO World Heritage — a 16th-century Portuguese city centre. Quiet, grand, unlike the rest of Goa.

highlights.name: Dudhsagar Falls day trip
highlights.note: The milk waterfall — one of India's tallest. Only accessible Oct–May (Jeep tours); closed in monsoon.

highlights.name: Candolim or Calangute beach morning walk
highlights.note: The wide North Goa beaches are best early before the umbrellas go up.

highlights.name: Night market or Saturday Market (Arpora/Anjuna)
highlights.note: Goa's flea markets: silver jewellery, clothing, Tibetan crafts, live music.

name: Palghar
bestMonths: Oct–Feb for the beaches; monsoon seas are rough
summary: Quiet Konkan beaches, forts and hot springs north of Mumbai. Lovely in winter; in monsoon the sea is rough, so lean on temples, springs and forts over swimming.

highlights.name: Kelva Beach
highlights.note: Long casuarina-lined beach. No swimming in monsoon, the sea is rough, but a fine walk.

highlights.name: Shirgaon Fort
highlights.note: A small coastal fort, green and quiet in the rains.

highlights.name: Vajreshwari hot springs and temple
highlights.note: Warm sulphur springs and a temple, a good rainy-day pick. Dress modestly.
